name: Source Fidelity review for Immune Clearance Of Senescent Cells bundle
summary: The staged source, study, finding, intervention, and outlook records stay within the cited PubMed and PMC source boundaries. The bundle correctly treats uPAR CAR T, NKG2D-CAR T, and anti-GD3 immunotherapy as preclinical animal or nonhuman-primate signals; uses perforin-deficient mice as immune-surveillance rationale rather than therapeutic immune enhancement; keeps HLA-E/NKG2A and CMV-antigen CD4 T-cell data as human mechanistic, tissue, or ex vivo evidence; and does not create treated-human aging efficacy claims.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 32555459 supports the staged foundational uPAR CAR T claim: uPAR was identified as a senescence-associated surface target, uPAR-specific CAR T cells ablated senescent cells in vitro and in vivo, and mouse lung adenocarcinoma and liver fibrosis models showed pathology-model benefit.
findings.why_it_matters: This source can anchor engineered immune senolysis, but only as preclinical disease-model evidence rather than treated-human aging efficacy.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the pathology-model, animal, and no-human-aging-efficacy boundaries.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMIDs 38267706 and 41291258 support the staged anti-uPAR CAR T aging-mouse claims: the 2024 study reports exercise-capacity and metabolic effects in physiological aging and high-fat-diet mouse models, and the 2026 study reports intestinal barrier, regeneration, inflammation, mucosal immune, microbiome, and fitness-related effects in aged mice.
findings.why_it_matters: These sources strengthen the animal_signal baseline, but they do not establish human target engagement, treated-human biomarkers, or functional benefit.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the mouse-only and cell-therapy translation limitations.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 37585504 supports the staged NKG2D-CAR T bridge: NKG2D ligands were upregulated on senescent cells, NKG2D-CAR T cells improved aging-associated pathology and physical performance in mouse models, and autologous human NKG2D-CAR T cells deleted naturally occurring senescent cells in aged nonhuman primates without observed adverse effects in the report.
findings.why_it_matters: The nonhuman-primate signal is translationally important, but it remains preclinical and should not be presented as treated-human efficacy.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; preserve the aged-mouse, nonhuman-primate, and no-human-aging-trial caveats.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 30575733 supports the premise that perforin-deficient mice accumulate more senescent cells and show accelerated aging phenotypes, while PMID 31160572 supports HLA-E/NKG2A immune-evasion biology and in-vitro blockade of senescent-cell immune inhibition in human skin and cell systems.
findings.why_it_matters: These sources support immune-surveillance rationale and human-mechanistic checkpoint biology, but perforin deficiency is not a therapeutic intervention and HLA-E/NKG2A blockade is not yet in-vivo human aging efficacy.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the loss-of-function, senolytic-rescue, in-vitro, and human-mechanistic boundaries.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 39730825 supports the staged GD3 claim: senescent cells upregulated GD3 to suppress NK-cell immunosurveillance, and anti-GD3 immunotherapy attenuated experimentally induced or age-related mouse lung and liver fibrosis plus age-related bone remodeling.
findings.why_it_matters: GD3 is a credible senescence immune-checkpoint target in the staged source, but the intervention evidence remains preclinical and mouse-centered.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the mouse-intervention and target-selectivity caveats.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 37001502 supports the staged human skin and ex vivo claim: senescent fibroblasts expressed HLA-II and HCMV-gB, cytotoxic CD4 T-cell recruitment was associated with lower senescent fibroblast burden in old skin, and skin-resident CD4 CTLs eliminated HCMV-gB-positive senescent fibroblasts in an HLA-II-dependent manner.
findings.why_it_matters: This is human-relevant immune-clearance mechanism evidence, but not an interventional treated-human trial or a stage-upgrading efficacy result.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; preserve the observational, ex vivo, CMV-history, and no-treated-human-efficacy limitations.
